root_text.an9.26:1.2: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca candikāputto rājagahe viharanti veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.an9.26:1.3: Tatra kho āyasmā candikāputto bhikkhū āmantesi (…):
root_text.an9.26:1.5: ‘yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno cetasā citaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ bhikkhuno kallaṁ veyyākaraṇāya—
root_text.an9.26:1.6: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’”ti.
root_text.an9.26:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ candikāputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an9.26:2.2: “na kho, āvuso candikāputta, devadatto bhikkhūnaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.an9.26:2.3: ‘yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno cetasā citaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ bhikkhuno kallaṁ veyyākaraṇāya—
root_text.an9.26:2.4: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an9.26:2.5: Evañca kho, āvuso, candikāputta, devadatto bhikkhūnaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.an9.26:2.6: ‘yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ bhikkhuno kallaṁ veyyākaraṇāya—
root_text.an9.26:2.7: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’”ti.
root_text.an9.26:3.1: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā candikāputto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an9.26:3.3: ‘yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno cetasā citaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ bhikkhuno kallaṁ veyyākaraṇāya—
root_text.an9.26:3.4: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’”ti.
root_text.an9.26:3.5: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ candikāputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an9.26:3.6: “na kho, āvuso candikāputta, devadatto bhikkhūnaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.an9.26:3.7: ‘yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno cetasā citaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ bhikkhuno kallaṁ veyyākaraṇāya—
root_text.an9.26:3.8: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an9.26:3.9: Evañca kho, āvuso candikāputta, devadatto bhikkhūnaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.an9.26:3.10: ‘yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ bhikkhuno kallaṁ veyyākaraṇāya—
root_text.an9.26:3.11: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’”ti.
root_text.an9.26:4.1: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā candikāputto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an9.26:4.3: ‘yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno cetasā citaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ bhikkhuno kallaṁ veyyākaraṇāya—
root_text.an9.26:4.4: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’”ti.
root_text.an9.26:4.5: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ candikāputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an9.26:4.6: “na kho, āvuso candikāputta, devadatto bhikkhūnaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.an9.26:4.7: ‘yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno cetasā citaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ bhikkhuno kallaṁ veyyākaraṇāya—
root_text.an9.26:4.8: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an9.26:4.9: Evañca kho, āvuso candikāputta, devadatto bhikkhūnaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.an9.26:4.10: ‘yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ bhikkhuno kallaṁ veyyākaraṇāya—
root_text.an9.26:4.11: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an9.26:5.1: Kathañca, āvuso, bhikkhuno cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti?
root_text.an9.26:5.2: ‘Vītarāgaṁ me cittan’ti cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.26:5.3: ‘vītadosaṁ me cittan’ti cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.26:5.4: ‘vītamohaṁ me cittan’ti cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.26:5.5: ‘asarāgadhammaṁ me cittan’ti cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.26:5.6: ‘asadosadhammaṁ me cittan’ti cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.26:5.7: ‘asamohadhammaṁ me cittan’ti cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.26:5.8: ‘anāvattidhammaṁ me cittaṁ kāmabhavāyā’ti cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.26:5.9: ‘anāvattidhammaṁ me cittaṁ rūpabhavāyā’ti cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.26:5.10: ‘anāvattidhammaṁ me cittaṁ arūpabhavāyā’ti cetasā cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti.
root_text.an9.26:5.11: Evaṁ sammā vimuttacittassa kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno bhusā cepi cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā cakkhussa āpāthaṁ āgacchanti, nevassa cittaṁ pariyādiyanti;
root_text.an9.26:5.12: amissīkatamevassa cittaṁ hoti ṭhitaṁ āneñjappattaṁ, vayaṁ cassānupassati.
root_text.an9.26:6.2: Tassassu aṭṭha kukkū heṭṭhā nemaṅgamā, aṭṭha kukkū upari nemassa.
root_text.an9.26:6.3: Atha puratthimāya cepi disāya āgaccheyya bhusā vātavuṭṭhi, neva naṁ saṅkampeyya na sampavedheyya;
root_text.an9.26:6.6: atha dakkhiṇāya cepi disāya āgaccheyya bhusā vātavuṭṭhi, neva naṁ saṅkampeyya na sampavedheyya.
root_text.an9.26:6.9: Evamevaṁ kho, āvuso, sammā vimuttacittassa bhikkhuno bhusā cepi cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā cakkhussa āpāthaṁ āgacchanti, nevassa cittaṁ pariyādiyanti;
root_text.an9.26:6.10: amissīkatamevassa cittaṁ hoti ṭhitaṁ āneñjappattaṁ, vayaṁ cassānupassati.
root_text.an9.26:7.5: manoviññeyyā dhammā manassa āpāthaṁ āgacchanti, nevassa cittaṁ pariyādiyanti;
root_text.an9.26:7.6: amissīkatamevassa cittaṁ hoti ṭhitaṁ āneñjappattaṁ, vayaṁ cassānupassatī”ti.

translation_text.an9.26:0.3: The Simile of the Stone Pillar
translation_text.an9.26:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.an9.26:1.3: There Venerable Candikāputta addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an9.26:1.4: “Reverends, Devadatta teaches the mendicants like this:
translation_text.an9.26:1.5: ‘When a mendicant’s mind is solidified by heart, it’s appropriate for them to say:
translation_text.an9.26:1.6: “I understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”’”
translation_text.an9.26:2.1: When he said this, Venerable Sāriputta said to him,
translation_text.an9.26:2.2: “Reverend Candikāputta, Devadatta does not teach the mendicants like that.
translation_text.an9.26:2.6: ‘When a mendicant’s mind is well consolidated by heart, it’s appropriate for them to say:
translation_text.an9.26:2.7: “I understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”’”
translation_text.an9.26:4.1: And for a third time Venerable Candikāputta addressed the mendicants …
translation_text.an9.26:4.5: And for a third time, Sāriputta said to him,
translation_text.an9.26:4.6: “Reverend Candikāputta, Devadatta does not teach the mendicants like that.
translation_text.an9.26:4.10: ‘When a mendicant’s mind is well consolidated by heart, it’s appropriate for them to say:
translation_text.an9.26:4.11: “I understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”’
translation_text.an9.26:5.1: And how is a mendicant’s mind well consolidated by heart?
translation_text.an9.26:5.2: The mind is well consolidated by heart when they know: ‘My mind is without greed.’
translation_text.an9.26:5.5: … ‘My mind is not liable to become greedy.’
translation_text.an9.26:5.6: … ‘My mind is not liable to become hateful.’
translation_text.an9.26:5.7: … ‘My mind is not liable to become deluded.’
translation_text.an9.26:5.8: … ‘My mind is not liable to return to rebirth in the sensual realm.’
translation_text.an9.26:5.9: … ‘My mind is not liable to return to rebirth in the realm of luminous form.’
translation_text.an9.26:5.10: … ‘My mind is not liable to return to rebirth in the formless realm.’
translation_text.an9.26:5.11: When a mendicant’s mind is rightly freed like this, even if compelling sights come into the range of vision they don’t occupy their mind.
translation_text.an9.26:5.12: Their mind is quite untainted. It is steady, imperturbable, observing disappearance.
translation_text.an9.26:6.1: Suppose there was a stone pillar, sixteen feet long.
translation_text.an9.26:6.2: Eight feet were buried underground, and eight above ground.
translation_text.an9.26:6.3: And violent storms were to blow up out of the east, the west, the north, and the south.
translation_text.an9.26:6.6: They couldn’t make it tremor and tremble and quake.
translation_text.an9.26:6.8: It’s because that stone pillar is firmly embedded, with deep foundations.
translation_text.an9.26:6.9: In the same way, when a mendicant’s mind is rightly freed like this, even if compelling sights come into the range of vision they don’t occupy their mind.
translation_text.an9.26:6.10: Their mind is quite untainted. It is steady, imperturbable, observing disappearance.
translation_text.an9.26:7.5: and ideas come into the range of the mind they don’t occupy the mind.
translation_text.an9.26:7.6: Their mind is quite untainted. It is steady, imperturbable, observing disappearance.”
